title: Languas melanocarpa
taxonomy: Languas melanocarpa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Zingiberaceae, commonly known as the ginger family. It is a member of the genus Languas, which is native to tropical Asia. The plant is a type of ginger that is prized for its unique flavor and aroma. Languas melanocarpa is closely related to other ginger species, such as Alpinia and Zingiber, and is often used in traditional medicine and cooking. The classification of Languas melanocarpa reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Zingiberaceae family.
